frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_accepttermsandcond: By creating an account you agree to the terms which can be found here https://www.frontier.co.uk/legal/terms-and-conditions
I agree to the Terms & Conditions
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_accountemailexists: An account tied to this email already exists
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_accountlinksuccess: Your Frontier account was successfully linked with your platform account. You can manage your Frontier account settings by visiting user.frontierstore.net
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_codesentdescriptio: We've sent a verification code to {EmailAddress}, please enter it below. (Please allow a few minutes for the email to arrive)
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_invaliddisplayname: A Display Name cannot contain any of the following characters: \ / : * ? " < > |
Display Name does not contain enough characters.
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_invalidlogindetail: No account matching these details. Please check details and try again.
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_invalidpassword: Password is not strong enough. Try increasing the length of your password, including numbers, and using both uppercase and lowercase letters.
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_resetemailtext: Visit the website above to reset your password manually
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_resetpassworddescr: Enter your email to send password reset instructions
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_resetpasswordsentd: If an account with this email exists, a password reset email will be sent to {EmailAddress}. Please use the link found there to reset your password and then login. (Please allow a few minutes for the email to arrive)
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_rudedisplayname: Please do not use any language that could be perceived as offensive or rude.
frontieraccountsignup.frontieraccountsignup_signupstartdescrip: To use online features you have to link your platform account to your Frontier account. You can use an existing Frontier account or create a new one.

cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_edgepan_tooltip: Moves the camera when the cursor is at the edge of the screen.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_freelooktoggle_tooltip:
Allows the camera to fly freely around your park. Useful in tight areas or for taking screenshots.

You can use [{#LogicalButton.GameCamera_FreelookMode#}] to toggle Freelook from within the game.

[{#LogicalButton.GameCamera_RotateLeft#}] and [{#LogicalButton.GameCamera_RotateRight#}] will raise and lower the camera height in Freelook.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_gamepad_invertx_firstp: Inverts X axis in Free Look cameras.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_gamepad_invertx_orbit_: Inverts X axis in Orbit-style cameras.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_gamepad_inverty_firstp: Inverts Y axis in Free Look cameras.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_gamepad_inverty_orbit_: Inverts Y axis in Orbit-style cameras.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_noclip_tooltip: Allows the camera to move through the terrain
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_physicsraycasts_toolti: Allows the camera to focus on objects like scenery and buildings in addition to terrain and paths.  Focusing on scenery affects camera speed and zoom limitations.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_presets_centre_tooltip:
This preset uses Centre Focused options and is most similar to strategy and management games. It works best in flat parks.

Default setting.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_presets_cursor_tooltip: This preset uses Mouse Focused options. It works best in parks with complex terrain.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_presets_hybrid_tooltip: This preset uses a mix of options. It offers precise control but requires careful handling.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_rmbdrag_grab_tooltip: Grabs a point in the park and move around that.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_rmbdrag_simple_tooltip: Pans the camera based only on the mouse movement.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_rmbdrag_supergrab_tool: Grabs a point in the park and moves, rotates, and zooms around the grab point.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_rotatefocus_tooltip: Determine what the camera rotates around.
cameraoptionsmenu.cameraoptionsmenu_zoomfocus_tooltip: Determine where the camera moves as you zoom in and out.

loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_1: The radial menu contains frequently used options and settings, changing to suit different situations
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_10: Some guests will only come to your park if certain requirements are met through advertising
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_11: Guest Price Tolerance determines what each guest will pay for the same ride
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_12: Increasing the price of a ride beyond its bargain price helps reduce the number of guests in its queue
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_13: Guests will only stay in small parks for a short time
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_14: Guests will choose rides which meet their personal Preferences more often
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_15: Guests will have better moods when scenery matches their Preferences
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_16: Guests will prefer rides under cover when it's raining, and rides that are outside when it's sunny
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_17: The best way to increase Park Rating is to improve the lowest rated metric in your rating breakdown
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_18: Changing shop menus will control which dietary requirements are catered for
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_19: You can use paths and scenery to create a variety of crossroads, plazas, and intersections - these make great Blueprints
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_2: Build paths over obstructions by using sloped and elevated walkways
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_20: Through the Frontier Workshop, you can visit other players' parks as a guest to get inspiration
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_21: Collaborators can work on the same Sandbox park one-at-a-time through the Frontier Workshop
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_22: Each coaster type has different utilities and special pieces available
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_23: Franchise players can compete on park leaderboards against other players
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_24: The Event Sequencer allows you to trigger animations, special effects, and more
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_25: You can get ideas on how to improve your park by reading Guest Thoughts
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_26: If your Pool Safety is low, guests will panic more often
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_27: Go to the Staff Schedule to assign mechanics to Mechanic Workshops and generate Research Points
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_28: Staff who work more than 4 hours in a day require a break, or will suffer a penalty to Morale
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_29: Staff Morale has a direct impact on how fast a staff member works and moves around
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_3: Camera and cursor settings have adjustable sensitivity in the Settings menu
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_30: Staff Zones divide the park up into large sections for staff assignment
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_31: To use a pool or swimsuit ride, guests buy Pool Passes at Guest Services, and get changed in Changing Rooms
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_32: Pool Prestige increases with pool size and occupancy, and is boosted by scenery, pool extras, and flumes
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_33: You can upload your own Blueprints to the Frontier Workshop and share them with the world
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_34: Multiple flumes can be built on the same Flume Platform
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_35: Some guests want to use Body-Dryer machines when they get soaked
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_36: Guests will only buy food and drinks that their dietary restrictions allow
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_37: Guest Services sell Priority Passes that let guests access priority queues
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_38: For very popular rides, try adding a Priority Pass queue to make more money
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_39: Check out the customisation options for paths and pools. You can change their material, colour, edge style, and turn supports on and off
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_4: Use the Edit tool to move or combine sections for existing path and pool shapes
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_40: Use the Stamp Builder tool to snap together paths and plazas with geometric patterns
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_41: Guests prefer rides within their Fear and Nausea tolerances
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_42: The path Line Builder tool is excellent at crossing gaps and connecting plazas
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_43: The Stamp Delete tool allows you to cut away precise stamp shapes from existing path and pools
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_44: Guests will pay more for rides with attractive or exciting scenery
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_45: You can decrease the time it takes for guests to board or unboard a ride by moving the ride entrance and exit
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_46: Coasters with fewer utility pieces have lower power costs
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_47: Each coaster type has different engineering constraints on its track that limit the angles you can build
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_48: You can turn the visibility of Track Supports on or off in the tracked ride's Utility Settings tab
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_49: Coasters and Flumes must complete a test run before they can be opened to guests
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_5: Guests will leave the park early if they cannot satisfy their Hunger, Thirst, and Toilet needs
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_50: Good coasters should generate some Fear, but too much Fear will put off guests
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_51: Use the Draw tool to fill in gaps in custom pool and path shapes
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_6: Always wear suncream on sunny days! Guests will leave the park early if they get sunburnt
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_7: A good Park Reputation is maintained through Guest Experience - the sum of the different moods felt by guests throughout their time in your park
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_8: Good moods encourage guests to buy souvenirs
loadingscreen.loadingscreen_tooltip_9: Guests want to eat and drink at different times after arriving, but they need a steady supply of food and drink over time
